title: Velvety Tunisian Herb Chicken Broth
description: A fragrant low-carb soup with tender chicken, aromatic herbs, and silky texture from sous-vide cooking and gelatin leaves.
instructions: Season the chicken thighs with salt, pepper, turmeric, and paprika.
Seal the chicken with minced garlic and diced onion in a vacuum bag.
Place the bag in the sous-vide bath and cook for 2 hours.
Heat a saucepan over medium, add olive oil, and sauté parsley, coriander, and mint until fragrant.
Add the sautéed herbs to the chicken broth and bring to a gentle simmer.
Remove the chicken from the bag, shred it, and return it to the pot.
Soak gelatin leaves in cold water for 10 minutes, crush them into flakes, and stir into the broth.
Simmer the soup for an additional 10 minutes to melt the gelatin fully.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs, and enjoy a velvety, low-carb delight.
